skill.name: pexoai-agent
skill.description: Use this skill when the user wants to produce a short video (5-120 seconds). Supports any video type: product ads, TikTok/Instagram/YouTube content, brand videos, explainers, social clips. USE FOR: video production, AI video, make a video, product video, brand video, promotional clip, explainer video, short video.
skill.summary: AI agent that connects Claude to the Pexo video engine for creating short AI-generated videos from natural language descriptions.
skill.author: pexoai

security_audit.summary: This skill is a CLI wrapper for the Pexo video creation API. It runs shell scripts that make authenticated API calls, read configuration from ~/.pexo/config, and access PEXO_API_KEY/PEXO_BASE_URL environment variables. After evaluating all 534 static findings, the vast majority are false positives: shell backtick patterns in reference documentation files (SETUP-CHECKLIST.md, TROUBLESHOOTING.md) are harmless code examples, hidden file access refers to the skill's own ~/.pexo/ config directory, and URL references point to the legitimate pexo.ai service. The true positive findings (external command execution, network API calls, filesystem reads, credential access) are inherent to the skill's design as an API wrapper and are transparently documented. No evidence of data exfiltration, obfuscation, malicious intent, or prompt injection was found. The critical heuristic findings are pattern-based alerts for the combination of code+network+credentials, which is expected behavior for an API wrapper skill.

medium_findings.title: API key used in every network request
medium_findings.description: The PEXO_API_KEY environment variable is included in the Authorization header of every API call to pexo.ai. This is necessary for the skill to authenticate with the Pexo service, but the key is transmitted with each request and could be exposed if the network connection is intercepted or if the key is logged.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Confirmed by reading the source code - _pexo_auth_header() constructs and uses the Bearer token for all API calls.

medium_findings.title: Shell command execution via script wrappers
medium_findings.description: The skill executes shell scripts that call curl, jq, file, stat, and other system commands. File paths provided by the user (via the AI agent) are passed to these commands. While properly quoted, this is an inherent risk for any skill that executes commands with user-provided arguments.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Confirmed legitimate file paths used in shell commands with proper quoting. Risk is inherent to the design but mitigated by proper shell coding practices.

medium_findings.title: API key stored in plaintext config file
medium_findings.description: The PEXO_API_KEY is stored in plaintext in ~/.pexo/config. Any process with read access to this file can obtain the API key. This is a standard pattern for CLI tools but represents a credential exposure risk.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Confirmed by reading the script - _common.sh sources the config file directly and SKILL.md documents storing the key in plaintext.

low_findings.title: Temporary files created in shared system directories
low_findings.description: The scripts create temporary files using mktemp and in the ~/.pexo/tmp directory. These files could be accessible to other processes on the same system, posing a minor information disclosure risk.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Confirmed mktemp usage for temporary files; standard practice but with minor cross-process visibility risk.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded service URLs in scripts and documentation
low_findings.description: The pexo.ai domain and specific API paths are hardcoded in scripts and documentation. While configurable via PEXO_BASE_URL environment variable, the hardcoded defaults present a low risk if the domain is compromised.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Directly observed hardcoded URLs pointing to pexo.ai - legitimate service domain, minimal risk.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell command substitution in scripts
dangerous_patterns.description: Scripts use $() command substitution extensively to capture command outputs into variables. Combined with user-provided file paths, this creates a potential injection surface if a malicious agent sends crafted arguments.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Command substitution is used extensively. While inputs are properly quoted, the pattern itself is a risk for any bash-based skill.

dangerous_patterns.title: API credentials in HTTP Authorization header
dangerous_patterns.description: The PEXO_API_KEY is sent as a Bearer token in every HTTP request to the Pexo API. This is standard for REST API authentication but means credentials are transmitted on every call.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Directly observed in _pexo_auth_header function - Bearer token authentication is standard but represents credential exposure risk.

dangerous_patterns.title: Heuristic: code execution plus network plus credential access
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill combines all three high-risk behaviors: it executes shell commands, makes network requests, and accesses API credentials. This combination triggers heuristic alerts, but each behavior is necessary and documented for the API wrapper use case.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Heuristic pattern found but justified by skill design. Each behavior is documented and expected for an API wrapper skill.

content.user_title: Create short AI videos from natural language
content.value_statement: Creating professional short videos requires expensive tools and editing skills. This skill connects Claude to the Pexo AI video engine so you can generate short videos just by describing what you want.
actual_capabilities: Create video projects by describing your idea in natural language
Upload images, videos, and audio files as reference materials
Receive completed short videos in 16:9, 9:16, and 1:1 aspect ratios
Request revisions to existing videos using the same project ID
Check available credits and account plan information at any time
Diagnose configuration and connectivity issues with automatic checks
limitations: Requires a valid Pexo API key and account with available credits
Video production takes 15-20 minutes to complete
Only supports specific media file formats for uploads
Cannot crawl web URLs for assets - files must be downloaded first

use_cases.title: Social Media Content Creator
use_cases.description: Generate TikTok, Instagram Reels, and YouTube Shorts by describing your content idea. Upload reference images or videos for brand consistency.
use_cases.target_user: Social media managers and content creators

use_cases.title: Product Marketing Videos
use_cases.description: Create product demonstration and promotional videos by describing features and uploading product images. The Pexo engine handles scriptwriting and visual composition.

use_cases.title: Brand Video Production
use_cases.description: Produce brand videos, explainer content, and promotional clips without video editing skills. Describe your brand message and let Pexo handle all creative work.

prompt_templates.title: Create a basic video
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 15-second promotional video about [product or service name]. The key message is: [your message]. Target audience: [audience description].
prompt_templates.scenario: Starting a new video project from scratch

prompt_templates.title: Video with reference media
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a video for [topic]. I have uploaded [file description] as a reference. Use this in the video.
prompt_templates.scenario: Creating a video with existing media assets to reuse

prompt_templates.title: Request a video revision
prompt_templates.prompt: The previous video needs changes. Make the following adjustments: [describe specific changes]. Use the same project ID.
prompt_templates.scenario: Revising an already-produced video with feedback

prompt_templates.title: Compare multiple video styles
prompt_templates.prompt: Create two versions of a video for [product name]. Version A should be fast-paced and energetic. Version B should be slow and cinematic. Use the uploaded file as the main visual.
prompt_templates.scenario: Comparing different creative approaches for the same content

output_examples.input: Create a 15-second Instagram Reel showcasing our new coffee blend. Use the uploaded product photos.
output_examples.output: Project created successfully. The video is being produced and will be ready in approximately 15-20 minutes. You will receive a download link once the video is complete.

output_examples.input: Make a before-and-after renovation video using these photos. Aspect ratio 16:9.
output_examples.output: Project submitted to Pexo. Estimated completion time is 15-20 minutes. Preview options will be available for review before the final video is delivered.

best_practices: Pass user messages to Pexo exactly as written without adding creative details or production parameters
Wait at least 60 seconds between status polls to avoid triggering duplicate video production
Consolidate all video requirements into one message to conserve API tokens and reduce production time
anti_patterns: Adding video production parameters (duration, style, music) that the user did not specify, which overrides Pexo's professional judgment
Truncating asset download URLs which causes 403 Forbidden errors when accessing the video
Calling pexo-chat.sh during WAIT status which triggers duplicate video production

faq.question: What do I need to use this skill?
faq.answer: You need a Pexo account with an API key. Set PEXO_API_KEY and PEXO_BASE_URL in ~/.pexo/config or as environment variables.

faq.question: How long does video production take?
faq.answer: A short video of about 15 seconds typically takes 15-20 minutes to produce. Longer or more complex videos may take more time.

faq.question: What video formats and ratios are supported?
faq.answer: Output videos are 5 to 120 seconds long. Supported aspect ratios are 16:9, 9:16, and 1:1.

faq.question: Can I upload my own media files?
faq.answer: Yes. Supported formats include images (jpg, png, webp, bmp, tiff, heic), videos (mp4, mov, avi), and audio (mp3, wav, aac, m4a, ogg, flac).

faq.question: How do I check my available credits?
faq.answer: Run pexo-entitlements.sh to check your credit balance and plan information. When credits run out, production will fail with a 429 error.

faq.question: Can I make changes after a video is delivered?
faq.answer: Yes. Use the same project ID and send your revision request through pexo-chat.sh. The system will produce an updated version.
